dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9265 IH: To amend the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to require participation in the summer EBT program as a condition of participating in the school lunch program, and for other purposes.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Carson (for himself, Ms. Norton, Mr. Cohen, Ms. Brown, and Ms. Titus) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To amend the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to require participation in the summer EBT program as a condition of participating in the school lunch program, and for other purposes.

subsection.text: Section 13A(a) of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act (42 U.S.C. 1762(a)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking electing to participate in such program and inserting described in paragraph (2);
by striking beginning with summer 2024 and annually;
by striking The Secretary shall and inserting the following:

paragraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (1) and (2) as subparagraphs (A) and (B); and
by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.header: States and covered Indian Tribal organizations

subparagraph.text: With respect to summers 2024, 2025, and 2026, States and covered Indian Tribal organizations described in this paragraph are States and covered Indian Tribal organizations electing to participate in the program established under paragraph (1).
With respect to summer 2027 and each summer thereafter, States and covered Indian Tribal organizations described in this paragraph are—

clause.text: States participating in the school lunch program under this Act; and

subclause.text: participating in the school lunch program under this Act; or
electing to participate in the program established under paragraph (1).

subsection.text: Section 13A(c)(1) of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act (42 U.S.C. 1762(c)(1)) is amended, in the matter preceding subparagraph (A), by striking elect to.

paragraph.text: Section 13A(g) of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act (42 U.S.C. 1762(g)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking Beginning in 2024 and inserting the following:

paragraph.text: With respect to the years 2024, 2025, and 2026

subparagraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: Beginning in 2027, each State required to participate in the program under this section shall comply with the requirements under section 13(n)(3).
Section 13(n) of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act (42 U.S.C. 1761(n)(2)) is amended—

clause.text: in the heading for such paragraph, by striking and beyond and inserting through summer 2026; and
in the matter preceding subparagraph (A), by striking Beginning in 2024 and inserting With respect to the years 2024, 2025, and 2026; and

subparagraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: Beginning in 2027, each State desiring to participate in the program under this section or required to participate in the summer EBT program under section 13A shall—

subparagraph.text: with respect to the program under this section, notify the Secretary by January 1 of each year of its intent to administer such program; and

with respect to the program under this section and the summer EBT program under section 13A, submit for approval by February 15 a management and administration plan for the applicable program for the fiscal year, which shall include, as applicable, the information described in subparagraphs (A) through (F) of paragraph (2).

subsection.text: Section 13A of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act (42 U.S.C. 1762(g)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating subsection (h) as subsection (i); and
by inserting after subsection (g) the following:

subsection.text: Beginning with summer 2027, a State that participates in the school lunch program under this Act shall participate in the program under this section.
